tipRules.tip: Johnston: Target sand flats with isolated grass in 16 to 21 feet. Rotate through multiple locations — some produce well above others. The real key is LiveScope; without it you're at a huge disadvantage. I saw about 90% of fish on the graph way out and could cast to them before they were under the boat.

Johnston: Use a 3/8-ounce tungsten teardrop-style weight, size 1 Gamakatsu drop shot hook, with shad-colored or green pumpkin soft plastics. Pair with 15-pound braid main line and 8-pound fluorocarbon leader (10-foot leader).
Johnston: Look for oddities like a sunken barrel or isolated object — they can be smallmouth magnets. Fish that group out one day may be gone the next, so keep covering water until you find a group.

knowledge.insight: Johnston: The real key on deep sand flats is Garmin LiveScope — without it you're at a huge disadvantage. You can see 90% of the fish way out and cast to them before they're under the boat.
Johnston: Smallmouth groups on sand flats are transient. You could find a group one day but the next day they were gone. Keep covering water until you come across a group of them.
Johnston: For deep smallmouth drop shot, use a 7-foot medium-light Daiwa Tatula Elite Dropshot rod, size 3000 Daiwa Exist spinning reel, 15-pound braid to 8-pound fluorocarbon leader (10 feet).
